description_long: This is a single, elongated pear with a slightly bulbous base tapering to a narrow neck and a short, dark brown stem. The skin is mottled yellow with brownish patches, indicating ripeness and some minor bruising. The surface appears matte with a subtle, natural texture typical of pears, showing some unevenness and small blemishes. The overall shape is asymmetrical, with one side more rounded and the other gently sloping. The stem is slightly curved and dry, contrasting with the smoother body. The pear's coloration and form suggest it is a common variety found in markets and fruit bowls.
description: A ripe, yellow-brown pear with a bulbous base, narrow neck, and a short, dark stem. The skin is matte and slightly textured with visible blemishes.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the pear, highlighting its bulbous base and tapering neck with the stem visible.
description_view_1: Top-down view focusing on the stem and the narrow neck, with the rounded base less visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the elongated body and rounded base, with the stem at the far left.
description_view_3: View from the base, showing the circular bottom and the pear's tapering shape toward the stem.
attribute_location: fruit bowl on a kitchen countertop
attribute_contextual_placement: resting on its side among other fruits
attribute_condition: ripe with minor blemishes and slight bruising
attribute_orientation: lying on its side with the stem extending outward
